assertions.reason: A newly created Mission session in Mission-GUI-TEST used the required CCH-GPT-5.4 model-selection flow, opened on the Mission route, and persisted explicit Mission metadata for the created session id.
evidence: Opened the real new-session entry for project `Mission-GUI-TEST`, selected Mission in SessionConfigPage (`session-mode-mission`), and kept Local workspace selected. Screenshots: `/Users/clive/dev/droi/.worktrees/droi--happy-yak-p5n5/.factory/tmp/foundation-metadata-session-config-2.png` and `/Users/clive/dev/droi/.worktrees/droi--happy-yak-p5n5/.factory/tmp/foundation-metadata-mission-selected.png`.

Before the first Mission message, opened the model picker and explicitly selected the `CCH-GPT-5.4` option. Screenshot: `/Users/clive/dev/droi/.worktrees/droi--happy-yak-p5n5/.factory/tmp/foundation-metadata-model-open.png`.

Submitted the initial prompt `foundation-metadata mission identity validation session. Confirm mission metadata persistence only.` and created Mission session `a901a190-5925-431e-8657-8db9bb502391`; the sidebar row rendered as `session-mission-a901a190-5925-431e-8657-8db9bb502391`. Screenshot: `/Users/clive/dev/droi/.worktrees/droi--happy-yak-p5n5/.factory/tmp/foundation-metadata-after-create.png`.
Immediately after creation, TanStack router state reported pathname `/mission` with match `/chat-layout/mission`, and MissionPage DOM was present (`data-testid="mission-page"`).

Persisted file `/tmp/droi-mission-e2e/sessions/a901a190-5925-431e-8657-8db9bb502391.json` was created for the same session id and contains `isMission: true`, `sessionKind: "mission"`, `interactionMode: "agi"`, `decompSessionType: "orchestrator"`, `autonomyLevel: "off"`, and model `custom:CCH-Code-GPT-5.4-4`.
`agent-browser errors` returned no reported console/runtime errors for the validated flow.

assertions.reason: After the Mission session existed, both a normal follow-up send and a generic UI settings update preserved the same session id, stayed on `/mission`, and kept persisted Mission metadata instead of downgrading the session to normal `spec/auto` semantics.
evidence: Sent a normal follow-up message in the same session `a901a190-5925-431e-8657-8db9bb502391` (`foundation-metadata follow-up send: confirm this same session stays mission-shaped after settings update.`). After the send, router state still reported `/mission`, MissionPage remained visible, and `session-mission-a901a190-5925-431e-8657-8db9bb502391` stayed active. Screenshot: `/Users/clive/dev/droi/.worktrees/droi--happy-yak-p5n5/.factory/tmp/foundation-metadata-real-followup.png`.

This validation-oriented Mission session surfaced AskUser cards during the follow-up flow, but while they were present and after completing them through the normal UI, the route still stayed `/mission` and the same Mission sidebar row remained active. Screenshots: `/Users/clive/dev/droi/.worktrees/droi--happy-yak-p5n5/.factory/tmp/foundation-metadata-after-followup.png` and `/Users/clive/dev/droi/.worktrees/droi--happy-yak-p5n5/.factory/tmp/foundation-metadata-after-askuser-complete.png`.

Performed a generic settings update from the normal InputBar controls by changing the auto-level selector from `Spec` to `Low`. After the update, the active session still rendered as Mission and the router still reported `/mission`. Screenshot: `/Users/clive/dev/droi/.worktrees/droi--happy-yak-p5n5/.factory/tmp/foundation-metadata-after-settings-update.png`.

After the settings update, persisted file `/tmp/droi-mission-e2e/sessions/a901a190-5925-431e-8657-8db9bb502391.json` still preserved `isMission: true`, `sessionKind: "mission"`, `interactionMode: "agi"`, `decompSessionType: "orchestrator"`; only `autoLevel` changed from `default` to `low`, matching the normal UI change.

Switched away to the normal session `f57df244-34af-42ed-85e6-c54d87ccca39` (route `/`) and then reselected `session-mission-a901a190-5925-431e-8657-8db9bb502391`; the app returned to `/mission`, confirming the session was still Mission-classified and not downgraded. Screenshot: `/Users/clive/dev/droi/.worktrees/droi--happy-yak-p5n5/.factory/tmp/foundation-metadata-returned-mission.png`.
`agent-browser errors` returned no reported console/runtime errors for the validated flow.

frictions: The Electron app uses TanStack memory-history, so the browser address stayed `http://localhost:5173/`; route proof for `/mission` versus `/` had to come from router state plus MissionPage DOM rather than the address bar.

Direct `agent-browser click` on the visible send button intermittently timed out in this Electron surface. Dispatching the real `[data-testid="chat-send"]` element click was needed to complete the same user-visible send flow reliably.

This Mission session is itself a validation/planning conversation, so follow-up sends produced intermediate AskUser prompts. I completed them through the normal UI and treated them as user-surface evidence rather than a blocker.
